title: lewisia pygmaea var. sierrae
intro: Lewisia pygmaea var. sierrae, also known as Sierra Bitterroot, is a rare and endangered perennial plant species native to the Sierra Nevada mountain range in California, USA. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a digestive aid, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic agent. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in tincture form, with some herbalists recommending it as a treatment for various ailments.
